metadata.name: Individual Types
metadata.description: Foundation-level ontology defining individual types across B2B and B2C contexts. B2B individual types subclass RRR-ONT roles with organisational relations. B2C individual types are standalone Foundation entities with no org hierarchy. ICP (VP-ONT) groups individuals into target segments; IND-ONT defines the types within.
metadata.purpose: Define who the customer actually is — individual archetypes for B2B organisational buyers (subclassing RRR, with org relation) and B2C consumers (standalone). Market/industry context follows Foundation patterns. Customer journeys are PE-Series processes referencing IND types as participants.
metadata.creator: OAA Generator
crossReferences: VP-ONT (ICP is the class/group; IndividualType populates it)
PE-ONT (Customer journeys are PE processes; IND types are participants)

entities.name: IndividualType
entities.description: Archetype of a real person who interacts with products/services. B2B: subclasses RRR role, has org relation. B2C: standalone Foundation entity, no org hierarchy. ICP (VP-ONT) groups IndividualTypes into target segments.

properties.name: channel
properties.description: B2B (has org relation, subclasses RRR), B2C (standalone), or B2B2C (hybrid)

properties.name: typeCategory
properties.description: From B2BTypeEnum, B2CTypeEnum, or B2B2CTypeEnum — must align with channel per DR-IND-001

properties.name: description
properties.description: What this individual type does and typical context

properties.name: decisionStyle
properties.description: How this individual makes purchase decisions

properties.name: rrrSubclass
properties.description: B2B only: RRR-ONT role this individual subclasses

entities.name: IndividualQualifier
entities.description: Structured criteria that qualify when an individual type applies. Criteria types align with Foundation ORG-CONTEXT patterns and extend for B2C.

entities.name: IndustryProfile
entities.description: Binds an IndividualType to an industry vertical. Industry/market follows Foundation patterns (ORG-CONTEXT-ONT). Instance data owned by PFI brand/sub-brand.

properties.name: industry
properties.description: Industry vertical — aligns with ORG-CONTEXT MarketSegment

properties.name: owningInstance
properties.description: PFI brand/sub-brand that owns this instance data

entities.name: PurchaseBehaviour
entities.description: How an individual type enters and navigates buying decisions. Describes behavioural tendencies. The customer journey process itself is PE-Series — PurchaseBehaviour describes how the individual participates in that PE process.

properties.name: buyerJourneyType
properties.description: How the individual enters the buying process

relationships.name: Has Qualifier
relationships.description: Links individual type to qualifying criteria

relationships.name: Has Industry Profile
relationships.description: Links individual type to industry-specific instantiation — instance data owned by PFI brand/sub-brand

relationships.name: Has Purchase Behaviour
relationships.description: Links individual type to typical buying behaviour

relationships.name: Subclasses RRR Role
relationships.description: B2B only: links individual type to RRR-ONT role it subclasses. Inherits RACI/RBAC context.

relationships.name: Has Org Relation
relationships.description: B2B only: links individual type to organisation type. B2C has no org relation.

relationships.name: Populates ICP
relationships.description: Links individual type to VP-ONT ICP. ICP is the class; IndividualType is the type within that class.

relationships.name: Maps to Market Segment
relationships.description: Links industry profile to Foundation ORG-CONTEXT-ONT MarketSegment

relationships.name: Maps to Domain Entity
relationships.description: Links individual type to a domain-specific entity in another ontology

relationships.name: Influenced By
relationships.description: Links an individual type to another that influences its decisions

designRules.name: Channel-Category Alignment
designRules.description: IndividualType.channel MUST align with typeCategory prefix: B2B → B2B_*, B2C → B2C_*, B2B2C → B2B2C_*

designRules.name: B2B Individuals Subclass RRR
designRules.description: B2B IndividualTypes MUST populate rrrSubclass with a valid RRR-ONT role and MUST have hasOrgRelation to org:Organization. They inherit RACI/RBAC from RRR.

designRules.name: B2C Individuals Are Standalone
designRules.description: B2C IndividualTypes MUST NOT have rrrSubclass, rrrSeniorityLevel, or hasOrgRelation. Standalone Foundation entities — individuals, not org positions.

designRules.name: ICP Is the Class, IND Is the Individual
designRules.description: VP-ONT ICP defines the target segment (class/group). IND-ONT IndividualType defines types within. Relationship: populatesICP. Do not duplicate ICP-level attributes in IndividualType.

designRules.name: Customer Journeys Are PE Processes
designRules.description: The customer journey (awareness→consideration→decision→adoption→advocacy) is a PE-Series process. IND-ONT defines PurchaseBehaviour (how they enter/navigate) but the journey process, stages, and orchestration belong in PE-Series. IndividualTypes are participants in PE journeys.

designRules.name: Industry and Market Follow Foundation Patterns
designRules.description: IndustryProfile.industry MUST align with ORG-CONTEXT-ONT MarketSegment definitions. Industry/market classification is Foundation — IND-ONT references, does not redefine.

designRules.name: Instance Data Ownership
designRules.description: IndustryProfile.owningInstance identifies the PFI brand/sub-brand owning the instance data. Core types (PFC) are generic archetypes. Instance types (PFI) are brand-specific.

designRules.name: VP-RRR Alignment Preserved
designRules.description: Standing VP↔RRR convention (Problem→Risk, Solution→Requirement, Benefit→Result) applies through IND. B2B types inherit via RRR subclass. Pain summaries should align with VP Problem statements.

businessRules.name: B2B Must Have RRR Subclass
businessRules.condition: IF IndividualType channel=B2B AND typeCategory in (B2B_Executive, B2B_Management, B2B_Professional)
businessRules.action: THEN rrrSubclass MUST reference a valid RRR-ONT role

businessRules.name: B2B Must Have Org Relation
businessRules.action: THEN hasOrgRelation MUST link to org:Organization

businessRules.name: B2C Must Not Have Org Relation
businessRules.action: THEN rrrSubclass, rrrSeniorityLevel, hasOrgRelation MUST be null

businessRules.name: At Least One Qualifier Required
businessRules.action: THEN MUST have >=1 IndividualQualifier via hasQualifier

businessRules.name: Industry Profile Must Have Owner
businessRules.action: THEN owningInstance MUST identify PFI brand/sub-brand

businessRules.name: Inheritor Must Have Contextual Trigger
businessRules.action: THEN >=1 qualifier MUST have criteriaType=Contextual describing inheritance trigger

examples.name: Serious Collector
examples.description: Passionate, knowledgeable private collector acquiring antiques as personal interest and long-term investment. Maintains insured collection, attends major fairs/auctions, established dealer relationships. Standalone individual — no org affiliation.
examples.motivations: Passion for period/category, investment growth, prestige, completing collections, provenance discovery
examples.painSummary: Authentication uncertainty, insurance adequacy, valuation currency, storage/conservation, succession planning

examples.seasonality: Peak: Jan-Mar (post-estate), Sep-Nov (autumn fairs); auction calendar driven
examples.regulatoryContext: CITES (ivory/endangered materials), Cultural Property (export controls), MLR 2017
examples.industrySpecificTraits: Provenance documentation essential. Values decade-long dealer relationships. Attends Olympia, TEFAF, Frieze Masters.

examples.influencers: Specialist dealers, auction house specialists, other collectors, museum exhibitions
examples.trustSignals: Provenance docs, auction guarantee, BADA/LAPADA membership, condition report
examples.channelPreference: Auction, specialist dealers, fairs, private treaty

examples.name: Inheritor / Beneficiary
examples.description: Individual who received antique items via inheritance/bequest/estate settlement. Unfamiliar with market, overwhelmed by valuation/insurance/sell-or-keep decisions. Standalone — no org.
examples.motivations: Understanding value, informed sell-or-keep, honouring legacy, insurance, navigating probate
examples.painSummary: No knowledge of value, emotional attachment, market unfamiliarity, exploitation risk, probate pressure

examples.regulatoryContext: Probate rules, inheritance tax (HMRC), capital gains on disposal
examples.industrySpecificTraits: Needs handholding. Values empathy over expertise. Often a gateway customer.

examples.influencers: Solicitors, family, estate agents, local auction houses
examples.trustSignals: RICS/BADA credentials, solicitor referral, transparent fees, no-obligation consult
examples.channelPreference: Local auction, estate valuer, solicitor referral

examples.name: Insurance Underwriter (Antiques Specialist)
examples.description: Specialist insurance professional assessing risk and underwriting high-value antique collections. Subclasses RRR CRO. Works within insurance company/Lloyd's syndicate — has org relation.
examples.motivations: Accurate risk pricing, claims ratio, portfolio growth, FCA/PRA compliance
examples.painSummary: Valuation reliability, fraud detection, coverage disputes, security documentation, market volatility

examples.seasonality: Annual renewal cycle; new business spikes around fair/auction seasons
examples.regulatoryContext: FCA ICOBS, Solvency II, Lloyds minimum standards, Consumer Duty
examples.industrySpecificTraits: Requires RICS valuations <3 years. Assesses security (alarms, safes, CCTV). Condition surveys for items >50k.

examples.influencers: Claims data, valuation reports, broker submissions, market loss ratios
examples.trustSignals: RICS/ASA accreditation, provenance, security survey, broker reputation
examples.channelPreference: Broker submission, valuer referral, industry networks

examples.name: Auction House Director
examples.description: Senior executive responsible for strategic direction, catalogue quality, and commercial performance of an auction house. Subclasses RRR CEO. Has org relation to auction house.
examples.motivations: Sale revenue, premium consignments, catalogue prestige, buyer growth, hammer-to-estimate ratio
examples.painSummary: Consignor competition, buyer fatigue, online/live balance, estimate accuracy, AML/CITES burden

examples.seasonality: Spring/autumn major sales; Christmas decorative arts; summer country house
examples.regulatoryContext: AML 5th Directive, CITES, Consumer Contracts, auction conduct rules
examples.industrySpecificTraits: Relationship-driven. Board-level platform decisions. Catalogue reputation existential. Online bidding now competitive differentiator.

examples.influencers: Board, department heads, IT director, peer houses, industry press
examples.trustSignals: Track record, peer refs, SLA guarantees, data security, white-label flexibility
examples.channelPreference: Direct vendor, SoFAA conferences, peer recommendation

examples.name: Hobbyist Collector
examples.description: Casual, passion-driven collector acquiring antiques for enjoyment. Moderate budget, growing knowledge, active in local fairs and online. Standalone individual.
examples.motivations: Enjoyment, learning, community, affordable finds, decorating with character
examples.painSummary: Authenticity uncertainty, storage, overpaying, finding reliable accessible dealers

examples.industrySpecificTraits: Price-conscious, condition-sensitive. eBay, Etsy, local auctions, antiques centres. Social media for identification.

examples.influencers: Social media, YouTube, dealer recs, collector friends
examples.trustSignals: Seller reviews, fair reputation, return policy, clear photos, honest condition
examples.channelPreference: Fairs, eBay, Etsy, antiques centres, local auction, charity shops

examples.name: Interior Designer
examples.description: Design professional sourcing antique pieces for client projects. Hybrid: buys from B2B suppliers for B2C clients. Intermediary role.
examples.motivations: Client brief fulfilment, period authenticity, trade margin, design coherence, sourcing exclusivity
examples.painSummary: Sourcing to brief, delivery timescales, condition surprises, client budget vs authenticity

examples.regulatoryContext: Consumer Rights Act (for client), trade description
examples.industrySpecificTraits: Needs quality images for client presentations. Values approval/hold/return. Expects 10-20% trade discount.

examples.influencers: Client brief, mood boards, dealer relationships, auction previews
examples.trustSignals: Trade discount, return policy, dealer reputation, photography quality, condition accuracy
examples.channelPreference: Specialist dealers, trade fairs, auctions, online platforms

examples.name: Antiques Investor
examples.description: Financially-motivated individual acquiring antiques as alternative assets. Portfolio diversification focus. Standalone individual — may use advisors but is the principal.
examples.motivations: Capital appreciation, diversification, inflation hedge, tangible assets, tax efficiency (chattels exemption)
examples.painSummary: Illiquidity, valuation volatility, storage/insurance costs, authentication risk, market timing

examples.regulatoryContext: CGT (chattels exemption <6000), IHT, AML for high-value
examples.industrySpecificTraits: Demands auction records, comparable sales, market indices. Values bonded storage. Interest in fractional ownership/tokenisation.

examples.influencers: Financial advisors, art analysts, auction specialists, fund managers, tax advisors
examples.trustSignals: Auction provenance, valuation history, price indices, dealer guarantees, independent auth
examples.channelPreference: Major auction houses, specialist dealers, art funds, private treaty
